demographics.living_situation: Lives at home in a two-storey house (bedroom upstairs, shower over the bath); adult child is present today but is not able to provide the level of physical support she now needs.

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I came home to get better, but I'm so weak I can't even stand up to make a cup of tea, and my legs are like jelly. Mum's nearly gone over twice trying to get to the toilet since she got home.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: She was discharged from hospital two days ago and instead of getting stronger she can barely stand, gets terribly dizzy when she gets up, and we're frightened she's going to have a bad fall at home.

symptom_narrative.onset: Started after she came home from a two-week hospital stay two days ago; the weakness has been there since discharge and is not improving.
symptom_narrative.duration: About 48 hours since getting home, no better.
symptom_narrative.character: Whole-body weakness, both legs feel like jelly; she is exhausted and breathless just moving from the bed to the chair.
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: She says she has never felt this weak in her life and won't try to stand on her own.
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: Both legs and generally all over — not one side.
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: Constant; worse when she first stands up, when she goes very dizzy.
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: Sitting back down and resting helps the dizziness settle.
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: Standing up quickly makes her head swim and her legs give way.
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: Very poor appetite since hospital and the family thinks she has lost weight; a bit slower answering questions than her normal self.
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: Nothing like this before — she was independent and doing her own shopping before this admission.
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: Cannot safely get to the bathroom, cannot transfer without help, now needs a walking frame she struggles to use, refusing to attempt to stand because she is afraid of falling.

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: Was in hospital for two weeks with a chest infection (pneumonia) and got home two days ago
past_medical_history.how_managed: Treated in hospital; told the chest infection had cleared up by discharge

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: A blood pressure tablet — the dose was put up recently
A course of antibiotic tablets started in hospital

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: Was fully independent before this admission; adult child present today but works and cannot stay to provide hands-on care; home has stairs and a shower over the bath.

objective_data_offered.value: Adult child reports she has gone from fully independent before hospital to unable to stand or transfer without help in the two days since discharge, and has nearly fallen twice trying to reach the toilet.
On video she looks pale, frail and worn out; her movements are slow and deliberate; she is gripping the arms of the chair and will not attempt to stand.
Family says she has barely eaten since hospital and they think she has lost weight over the admission.
Family checked her temperature at home with the ear thermometer and say it read normal — no fever.
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: Patient-stated home thermometer reading, not a clinician measurement.

objective_data_offered.value: Adult child says she gets very dizzy and her head swims for a few seconds each time she stands up, then it settles when she sits back down.
